title: How to pass --cgroup-parent to podman container
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I am testing my application with podman and trying to change the parent group mount point to /docker. By default, PODMAN provides /machine.slice/libpod-* but I want some other cgroup mount. I tried using the following but it is not working:

```
podman create \
  --hostname test1 \
  --cgroup-parent="/ol7" \
  --name test1 \
oraclelinux:7
```
The above command kept on failing. How can I change the cgroup parent mountpoint inside the container?

**Describe the results you received:**
It fails with the following error:
```
Error: did not receive systemd slice as cgroup parent when using systemd to manage cgroups: invalid argument
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
It should work with the different cgroup mount point. It works in docker and we can pass different parent cgroup to docker daemon but here podman doesn't have any daemon and not sure how can I pass --cgroup-parent?

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.9
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

It is a virtual machine.

comments.body:
Further update on this, I tried following and able to create the container but it is failing on start:

```
# podman create   --hostname test1  --cgroup-parent=/ol7  --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --name test1   oraclelinux:7
a5819c3f7c22798473e8c334f59bad3f2b5d4b52b3cd94d7a4c4855cbb6d2db8
# podman start test1
Error: unable to start container "test1": container_linux.go:345: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:281: applying cgroup configuration for process caused \"Invalid slice name ol7\""
: OCI runtime error
```

looks like we need `--cgroup-manager=cgroupfs` for both commands.  Can you try:

```
# podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs create ...
# podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs start -l
```

I tried but it doesn't work. I am wondering do we need to create ol7 cgroup ? Isn't be created by podman?

```
#  podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs create   --hostname test1  --cgroup-parent=/ol7   --name test1   oraclelinux:7
1f459709dfbbf2ab27915b62c8270686691fe47e2e023f9cdaf03e75a41456d5
# podman start test1 --latest
unable to start container "test1": container_linux.go:345: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:281: applying cgroup configuration for process caused \"Invalid slice name ol7\""
: OCI runtime error
Error: unable to start container "1f459709dfbbf2ab27915b62c8270686691fe47e2e023f9cdaf03e75a41456d5": container_linux.go:345: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:281: applying cgroup configuration for process caused \"Invalid slice name ol7\""
: OCI runtime error
```

@giuseppe 

It worked as per your suggestion:
```
podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs start -l test1
```

@giuseppe 

Though I am able to start the container,  I still have some question. How do I set the cpu.rt_runtime_us to 950000 during the group creation because if  I pass --cpu-rt-runtime=95000 for test1,  the parent group doesn't have this value. With docker I was able to do it bt passing the --cpu-rt-runtime to docker-daemon and child cgroup can inherit the values from the parent cgroups.

For details, please check following:
```
# pwd
/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu,cpuacct/ol7
# cat cpu.rt_runtime_us 
0
```

Trying with another cgroup:
```
# podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs create   --hostname test1  --cgroup-parent=/ol71   --cpu-rt-runtime=95000 --name test11   oraclelinux:7
8dbbcbdacdcb6327b1e35dcdbf2d1b098cc893e1a5f3b78dc8f5ce4c3aa8b522
# podman --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs start -l test11
unable to start container "test11": container_linux.go:345: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:281: applying cgroup configuration for process caused \"failed to write 95000 to cpu.rt_runtime_us: write /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu,cpuacct/ol71/libpod-8dbbcbdacdcb6327b1e35dcdbf2d1b098cc893e1a5f3b78dc8f5ce4c3aa8b522/cpu.rt_runtime_us: invalid argument\""
: OCI runtime error
Error: unable to start container "8dbbcbdacdcb6327b1e35dcdbf2d1b098cc893e1a5f3b78dc8f5ce4c3aa8b522": container_linux.go:345: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:281: applying cgroup configuration for process caused \"failed to write 95000 to cpu.rt_runtime_us: write /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu,cpuacct/ol71/libpod-8dbbcbdacdcb6327b1e35dcdbf2d1b098cc893e1a5f3b78dc8f5ce4c3aa8b522/cpu.rt_runtime_us: invalid argument\""
: OCI runtime error
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@giuseppe Could you answer the question so we can close this issue?
I don't think you can do it directly from Podman.  If you want to configure the parent cgroup, you'd have to do it manually.

how to work in centos8 podman run with cgroup-parent option?

# podman run --replace --name cg-redis -itd --cgroup-parent=/cgtest --rm -p 16379:6379 docker.io/library/redis --appendonly yes

If you are not using systemd-managed slices as the cgroup parent you need to use the `cgroupfs` cgroup manager (`--cgroup-manager=cgroupfs`).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since the question was answered I am closing.  If this is still a problem please reopen.
